question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Pool{MaxLifetime}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests timed to arrive just as Pool.MaxLifetime connection recycling occurs, causing a burst of simultaneous connection teardowns and re-establishments that temporarily saturates the DB connection pool and increases node CPU and network I/O by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Response{MaxTransactionsInBlock}] Can an unprivileged external user identify blocks with transaction counts near MaxTransactionsInBlock and repeatedly request them, causing the server to perform maximum-size JSON marshaling on every request and increasing CPU and memory allocation by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Http{WriteTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user request large block responses (near MaxTransactionsInBlock) and then stall TCP acknowledgments, causing the server to buffer the full response in memory until Http.WriteTimeout expires, and by doing this across many connections simultaneously increase node memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Cache{MaxSize}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the single EntityCacheKey='entity' namespace by sending requests for entities with IDs that hash to the same cache bucket, causing hash collision chains that degrade cache lookup from O(1) to O(n) and increase CPU consumption per request by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Db{Pool}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that each open a transaction and hold it open (e.g., by sending a partial request that keeps the DB transaction alive), consuming Pool connections and causing other requests to wait, which increases goroutine count and memory consumption by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Config{Online}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Online=true mode to trigger network-dependent operations (e.g., node health checks or address book fetches) on every relevant API call, causing repeated outbound network connections and DNS lookups that increase node CPU and network I/O by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user send /network/options or /network/status requests that cause the server to serialize the entire NodeMap (map[string]hiero.AccountID) into the response on every call, and by sending a sustained stream of such requests increase CPU and memory allocation by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Http{IdleTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the difference between Http.IdleTimeout and Http.ReadTimeout to hold connections in the idle state (after completing one request) without sending a second request, causing the server to maintain per-connection goroutines and buffers and increasing memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Pool{MaxOpenConnections}] Can an unprivileged external user send exactly Pool.MaxOpenConnections (100) concurrent requests that each trigger a slow DB query, saturating the connection pool and causing all subsequent requests to block in a wait queue, which causes goroutine stack memory to accumulate and increases node memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Config{NodeRefreshInterval}] Can an unprivileged external user send API requests that indirectly trigger NodeRefreshInterval-based address book DB queries by causing the refresh timer to fire more frequently (e.g., through clock manipulation or by exploiting timer reset logic), causing repeated full address book scans that increase DB and node CPU consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Cache{MaxSize}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that reference valid but rarely-accessed entity IDs, causing the entity cache to fill with cold entries that displace hot entries, increasing the cache miss rate for legitimate traffic and causing a sustained increase in DB query load exceeding 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Db{StatementTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests that trigger DB queries with expensive JOIN operations or large table scans, causing each query to consume significant DB CPU for up to StatementTimeout (20s) before timing out, and by sending a moderate number of such requests increase overall node and DB resource consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Response{MaxTransactionsInBlock}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxTransactionsInBlock limit by requesting blocks at the boundary of this limit, causing the server to allocate and populate maximum-size transaction slices on every such request, and by pipelining many such requests increase heap allocation rate and GC pressure by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Http{ReadHeaderTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user send HTTP/1.1 requests with an abnormally large number of headers (each sent slowly), causing the server to allocate header map entries and hold the connection open until Http.ReadHeaderTimeout, and by doing this across many connections simultaneously increase memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Pool{MaxIdleConnections}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests in a pattern that causes the DB pool to oscillate between idle and active states, repeatedly triggering connection creation and destruction beyond Pool.MaxIdleConnections, causing sustained TCP and TLS overhead that increases node CPU and network I/O by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Config{Cache}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Config.Cache is a map[string]Cache (keyed by arbitrary strings) with no validation on the number of cache namespaces, and if the application dynamically creates cache instances per key, cause memory exhaustion by triggering requests that reference many distinct cache keys?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to endpoints that perform NodeMap lookups on every call, and if the NodeMap contains many entries, cause repeated map iteration overhead that increases CPU consumption per request and cumulatively raises node resource consumption by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Http{WriteTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Http.WriteTimeout by requesting endpoints that produce large responses and then reading the response at the minimum possible rate, causing the server to hold the response buffer in memory for the full WriteTimeout duration and increasing node memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Db{Pool}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger DB queries returning large result sets, causing the Go DB driver to allocate large row-scan buffers that are not released until the connection is returned to the pool, and by holding many such connections simultaneously increase node heap memory by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Config{NodeRefreshInterval}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the node address book to be re-fetched before NodeRefreshInterval expires (e.g., by triggering an error path that resets the refresh timer), causing more frequent DB queries for the address book and increasing node DB CPU consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Cache{MaxSize}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for entities with IDs that are valid but not present in the DB, causing cache misses that result in DB queries returning empty results, and by sending a sustained stream of such requests increase DB query rate and node CPU consumption by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Http{IdleTimeout}] Can an unprivileged external user exploit HTTP/2 multiplexing (if enabled) to open many streams over a single connection within the Http.IdleTimeout window, causing the server to allocate per-stream state for each concurrent stream and increasing memory consumption by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Pool{MaxOpenConnections}] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that each trigger a DB query with a large ORDER BY or GROUP BY clause, causing the DB to allocate sort buffers for each connection up to Pool.MaxOpenConnections, and by saturating the pool increase both DB and node memory consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Response{MaxTransactionsInBlock}] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests for the same high-transaction-count block, causing the server to perform redundant DB queries and JSON serialization for each request (if caching is not applied at the response level), increasing CPU and memory consumption by more than 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: Config{Log}] Can an unprivileged external user trigger API error paths (e.g., by sending malformed but parseable requests) that cause the server to emit verbose log entries at the configured Log.Level, and if the log level is 'debug' or 'trace', cause sustained high-volume log I/O that increases node CPU and disk I/O by more than 30%?
